slice: A direct follow-up: "can we show the thumbnails in the cloud worlds." _cloudWorldCard() previously only ever showed a plain glyph icon, never a real authored thumbnail image, unlike the local "My World Projects" cards which already resolve thumbnail.png via ProjectModel.getAsset(). Confirmed this was a real oversight, not a constraint -- ProjectSync.push() stores the full raw Project object (including .files) verbatim, so a Cloud World row's data is already thumbnail-resolvable with no translation. Fixed by reusing the identical getAsset/fallback/onerror-degrades-to-glyph pattern the local project card already uses; no CSS change needed since the shared .wb-project-thumb img rule already applies. Since this changes worldBuilderApp.js's own content, the ?v= cache-bust query param on every tag in index.html was bumped from 0095 to 0097 to match, per the established ritual -- skipping it would have reintroduced the exact stale-cache bug the prior sprint fixed. Verified via a new 8-assertion Playwright suite (module-body route mocking, the established pattern for this feature) plus full regression across the existing 13/15/8-assertion suites and both goldenBuild.js suites, all unchanged. Confined to js/worldBuilderApp.js and index.html.
